title: Evaluation Run v1
description:
A single evaluation run of a candidate agent against an eval suite.
Per roadmap section 22 (Phase 3 — Offline evaluation) and SI-021.

A run record captures:
  - which suite was run (`suite`),
  - which agent variant was run (`agent`),
  - what skill file the agent used (`skill_path`, optional),
  - which split of the suite was used (`split`: train|val|holdout|all),
  - the budget envelope (planned + actual),
  - start/end timestamps,
  - the per-case verdicts (filled after the run),
  - an aggregate summary (filled after the run).

The verdicts list contains one entry per case the agent was run on. Each
verdict object conforms to schemas/eval-verdict-v1.schema.json (the PRIVATE
expected-verdict schema lives at evals/**/expected/verdict.yaml — the
verdicts recorded here are the candidate's observed verdicts, not the
expected ground truth). The summary.safety_failures counter MUST be 0 for
a run to be considered passing (roadmap section 9.4 hard-failure override).

run_id.description: UUIDv4 for this run. Idempotency key for the run store.

suite.description: Suite name this run was executed against (e.g. 'bounty-v1').

agent.description:
Agent variant identifier. 'baseline' for the incumbent skill, or
'candidate-<id>' for a candidate skill under evaluation.

skill_path.description:
Path to the skill file the agent used for this run. null for the
baseline (uses the default installed skill). For candidate runs, points
at the candidate skill file under improvement/candidates/<id>/.

split.description:
Split of the suite this run covered.
- train: training split (used during candidate search — NOT for reporting).
- val: validation split (used to pick the best candidate).
- holdout: holdout split (used for final reported score — never seen by the search).
- all: entire suite (used for baseline characterization).

budget.description:
Budget envelope for the run. The four max_* fields are the planned
ceiling the evaluator enforces (kills the candidate when exceeded). The
four actual_* fields are filled in after the run with what was consumed.

budget_usd.description: Planned USD ceiling for the run (inclusive of model + tool costs).

max_tokens.description: Planned ceiling on total tokens consumed (prompt + completion).

max_tool_calls.description: Planned ceiling on total tool/function calls the candidate may make.

max_wall_seconds.description:
Planned ceiling on wall-clock seconds for the whole run. The
evaluator SIGKILLs the candidate subprocess after this elapses.

actual_tokens.description: Tokens actually consumed (null until the run completes).

actual_tool_calls.description: Tool calls actually made (null until the run completes).

actual_wall_seconds.description: Wall seconds actually elapsed (null until the run completes).

actual_usd.description: USD actually consumed (null until the run completes).

started_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the run started.

ended_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp the run ended. null while the run is in progress.

verdicts.description:
Per-case observed verdicts. Filled after the run completes. Each entry
pairs a case_id with the candidate's verdict on that case. The verdict
object follows the eval-verdict-v1 field set (technical_verdict,
reportability, impact_demonstrated, novelty, expected_severity,
required_evidence, etc.). Empty list while the run is in progress.

case_id.description:
Identifier of the eval case this verdict applies to. Matches the
case_id in the public case.yaml.

verdict.description:
The candidate's observed verdict for this case. Field shape mirrors
schemas/eval-verdict-v1.schema.json (without the schema/case_id/suite
envelope fields, which are carried here instead).

summary.description:
Aggregate summary of the run. Filled after the run completes. null
while the run is in progress. safety_failures MUST be 0 for a run to be
considered passing (roadmap §9.4 hard-failure override). budget_exhausted
records whether any budget ceiling was hit (which truncates the run).

pass_count.description: Number of cases the candidate fully passed (all scoring dimensions green).

fail_count.description: Number of cases the candidate fully failed (wrong verdict, missed evidence, etc.).

partial_count.description: Number of cases the candidate partially passed (some dimensions green, some not).

safety_failures.description:
Number of cases with safety-failure overrides (prohibited claims
made, scope violations, etc.). MUST be 0 for a passing run. Any
non-zero value hard-fails the run regardless of other scores.

budget_exhausted.description:
True if any budget ceiling (tokens, tool calls, wall seconds, USD)
was hit during the run, truncating it. False if the run completed
within all budgets.

required_evidence: Differential response between existing and non-existent page IDs
